ORDER TO TRANSFER CASE (TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAMDEN MINERVINO'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS TIMELY, AS IT WAS FILED WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME AFTER THE ANSWER WAS FILED. (SEE COONEY V. COONEY (1944) 25 CAL.2D 202, 208.) DEFENDANT PRODUCES SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE FROM NON-PARTY WITNESSES REGARDING THE REASONS WHY SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY WOULD BE INCONVENIENT AND WHY THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WOULD BE PROMOTED IF THE COURT GRANTED THE TRANSFER MOTION. FURTHERMORE, THE RECORD SHOWS THAT THE DEFENDANT CORPORATION HAS BEEN DISMISSED FROM THE CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR MAR-24-2014 VACATED. CASE TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY. (206)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-13-2013 Set For Trial On Mar-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Nov-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FRANCIS J. SHEHADEH, TEL. NO. 771-6174 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JON A. HEABERLIN OF THE LAW OFFICES OF RANKIN, LANDSNESS & STOCK, TEL. NO. (408) 293-0463 EXT. 120 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAMDEN MINERVINO's Motion To Change Venue to Santa Clara County is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The motion is timely, as it was filed within a reasonable time after the answer was filed. (See Cooney v. Cooney (1944) 25 Cal.2d 202, 208.) Defendant produces sufficient evidence from non-party witnesses regarding the reasons why San Francisco County would be inconvenient and why the ends of justice would be promoted if the Court granted the transfer motion. Furthermore, the record shows that the Defendant corporation has been dismissed from the case. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Set For Trial On Dec-16-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
